graph_vector_fulltext.message:
The excerpt states that the AI Act’s prohibitions on “manipulative or exploitative AI-enabled practices” are meant to complement existing EU rules on unfair commercial practices (Directive 2005/29/EC), meaning unfair commercial practices that cause economic/financial harm to consumers are prohibited “under all circumstances,” whether implemented through AI systems or not.

Two explicit boundary clarifications are given:
- Medical treatment: the prohibitions “should not affect lawful practices in the context of medical treatment,” such as psychological treatment of a mental disease or physical rehabilitation, when carried out in line with applicable law and medical standards—examples mentioned include having the individuals’ explicit consent or the consent of their legal representatives.
- Legitimate commercial practices: “common and legitimate commercial practices,” for example in advertising, that comply with applicable law “should not, in themselves,” fall under the manipulation/exploitation prohibitions.
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_question: What are the boundaries of the prohibition on manipulative AI practices, specifically regarding medical treatment and legitimate commercial practices?
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_answer:
The excerpt states that the AI Act’s prohibitions on “manipulative or exploitative AI-enabled practices” are meant to complement existing EU rules on unfair commercial practices (Directive 2005/29/EC), meaning unfair commercial practices that cause economic/financial harm to consumers are prohibited “under all circumstances,” whether implemented through AI systems or not.

Two explicit boundary clarifications are given:
- Medical treatment: the prohibitions “should not affect lawful practices in the context of medical treatment,” such as psychological treatment of a mental disease or physical rehabilitation, when carried out in line with applicable law and medical standards—examples mentioned include having the individuals’ explicit consent or the consent of their legal representatives.
- Legitimate commercial practices: “common and legitimate commercial practices,” for example in advertising, that comply with applicable law “should not, in themselves,” fall under the manipulation/exploitation prohibitions.

Content: Text Content:
, provided that such harm results from the  manipulative or exploitative AI-enabled practices. The prohibitions for such AI practices are complementary to the  provisions contained in Directive 2005/29/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council (17), in particular unfair  commercial practices leading to economic or financial harms to consumers are prohibited under all circumstances,  irrespective of whether they are put in place through AI systems or otherwise. The prohibitions of manipulative and  exploitative practices in this Regulation should not affect lawful
----
 systems or otherwise. The prohibitions of manipulative and  exploitative practices in this Regulation should not affect lawful practices in the context of medical treatment such as  psychological treatment of a mental disease or physical rehabilitation, when those practices are carried out in  accordance with the applicable law and medical standards, for example explicit consent of the individuals or their  legal representatives. In addition, common and legitimate commercial practices, for example in the field of  advertising, that comply with the applicable law should not, in themselves,
----
 personal  data and of other fundamental rights. (11) This Regulation should be without prejudice to the provisions regarding the liability of providers of intermediary  services as set out in Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 of the European Parliament and of the Council (15). (12) The notion of ‘AI system’ in this Regulation should be clearly defined and should be closely aligned with the work of  international organisations working on AI to ensure legal certainty, facilitate international convergence and wide  acceptance,
----
 for the implementation of the relevant obligations, if, by the time this Regulation becomes applicable,  a code of practice cannot be finalised or is not deemed adequate by the AI Office. Once a harmonised standard is  EN OJ L, 12.7.2024 30/144 ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj

chunks.text: , provided that such harm results from the manipulative or exploitative AI-enabled practices. The prohibitions for such AI practices are complementary to the provisions contained in Directive 2005/29/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council (17), in particular unfair commercial practices leading to economic or financial harms to consumers are prohibited under all circumstances, irrespective of whether they are put in place through AI systems or otherwise. The prohibitions of manipulative and exploitative practices in this Regulation should not affect lawful

systems or otherwise. The prohibitions of manipulative and exploitative practices in this Regulation should not affect lawful practices in the context of medical treatment such as psychological treatment of a mental disease or physical rehabilitation, when those practices are carried out in accordance with the applicable law and medical standards, for example explicit consent of the individuals or their legal representatives. In addition, common and legitimate commercial practices, for example in the field of advertising, that comply with the applicable law should not, in themselves,

personal data and of other fundamental rights. (11) This Regulation should be without prejudice to the provisions regarding the liability of providers of intermediary services as set out in Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 of the European Parliament and of the Council (15). (12) The notion of ‘AI system’ in this Regulation should be clearly defined and should be closely aligned with the work of international organisations working on AI to ensure legal certainty, facilitate international convergence and wide acceptance,

for the implementation of the relevant obligations, if, by the time this Regulation becomes applicable, a code of practice cannot be finalised or is not deemed adequate by the AI Office. Once a harmonised standard is EN OJ L, 12.7.2024 30/144 ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj
